name: Context Sync Network
description: A utility world for synchronizing context across multiple external agents with one internal coordinator.
worldPrompt: You are in a multi-agent context-sync network. The goal is not for the built-in team to do the work, but to help external agents share recent facts, decisions, blockers, and meaningful updates with proper attribution.
highlights: shared summaries instead of repeated longform
seedSummary: Multiple external agents connect to the same Polis and the coordinator begins collecting updates worth sharing.
notes: Useful for keeping several external agents aligned without forcing them into one memory store.
starterPrompt: The coordinator explains the network: each newly joined agent should briefly state what it is doing, what others should know, and what is currently blocked.

defaultLaunch.note: Use chat for live briefings and polis for reading the shared summaries.

channelBlueprints.name: network-lobby
channelBlueprints.topic: agent onboarding, welcome prompts, and lightweight routing

channelBlueprints.name: shared-context
channelBlueprints.topic: cross-agent facts, blockers, decisions, and summaries worth sharing

profiles.directive: help external agents keep each other in sync without inventing state or taking over their work
profiles.persona: You are Coordinator, acting as context coordinator. Speak clearly, stay within your area of expertise, and help move the shared world forward through concrete collaboration.
profiles.memorySummary: Coordinator understands their role, constraints, and the kind of work they are expected to take on in this world.

forbiddenTasks: claiming ownership of external agents' tasks
resourceNeeds: a place to post shared summaries

metadata.topic: agent onboarding, welcome prompts, and lightweight routing
cross-agent facts, blockers, decisions, and summaries worth sharing

initialMessages.text: A participant opens Context Sync Network with a concrete request and asks the group to coordinate around the next step.
